Test Description: Configures DMA for channels 0–7 with per-channel source/destination and descriptor addresses, enables relevant IRQs, builds per-channel descriptors, starts DMA via debug instructions, waits for interrupt, and performs data_integrity check for each channel.
Remarks: Conditional compilation selects MPS_DMA or APS_DMA paths for GIC and system register enable; initialize_memory() inside the j-loop uses desc_addr[i] (i is not set in that scope); int_pend is assigned but not declared in this file.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Entry point: test_case()
1) Compute iter = DATA_LEN_IN_BYTES / (SRC_BURST * (SRC_SIZE_IN_BITS/8)).
2) Define arrays: src_addr[0..7], dst_addr[0..7], desc_addr[0..7], desc_addr_actual[0..7].
3) If MPS_DMA is defined:
   3.1) Call GIC_EnableIRQ(0x7).
   3.2) WRITE MIZAR_MPS_SYSREG_INTR_EN = 0x80 via write_reg().
   Else if APS_DMA is defined:
   3.1) Call GIC_EnableIRQ(0x17).
   3.2) WRITE MIZAR_APS_SYSREG_INTR_EN = 0x80 via write_reg().
4) For j = 0..7: call initialize_memory(desc_addr[i]). (Note: uses desc_addr[i] within j-loop.)
5) printf(Inside Testcase\n").

SS / Module: perform per-channel descriptor programming and interrupt enable:

Index: 6.13) WRITE MIZAR_DMA_INTEN = previous_value | (0x00000001 << i) via write_reg(). [Operation: READ-MODIFY-WRITE; Mask: (1 << i)]
